codeReview.summary: The feature adds the requested helpers and an integration test file, but the trigger-ordering implementation does not satisfy the values_quant requirement from VAL-UPSERT-009. deep_upsert only ensures an obj_desc_inst row exists for the object and never creates the missing obj_desc_quant/obj_desc_cat prerequisite rows, while the test suite only exercises the direct obj_desc_quant path and therefore misses the regression.

issues.description: VAL-UPSERT-009 is still unimplemented for values_quant/values_cat. deep_upsert only calls _ensure_obj_desc_inst(), and that helper returns once ANY obj_desc_inst row exists for the object (generic_ingest.py:785-788). But values_quant requires both the specific (object, desc_inst) row in obj_desc_inst and the matching (object, desc_quant) row in obj_desc_quant before insert (sql/tables.sql:571-576). Because no code creates the obj_desc_quant/obj_desc_cat prerequisite row here, a fresh values_quant insert can still fail even though the feature claims this prerequisite chain is automatic.

The VAL-UPSERT-009 test does not cover the contract it is named after. It validates direct obj_desc_quant insertion (test_deep_upsert.py:447-504), but the validation contract requires deep_upsert(values_quant, ...) to succeed without manually creating obj_desc_inst/obj_desc_quant. Because the test never exercises values_quant, it misses the broken prerequisite path in generic_ingest.py.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The repo-wide external-URL verification step is stale for this mission. The feature metadata still says `rg 'amazonaws|cassava\.ucsd' test/` should return no matches, but the repository already contains pre-existing cassava URLs in test_ingest_f006.py, so workers have to narrow the grep to their newly added test files instead.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json:257 says `rg 'amazonaws|cassava\.ucsd' test/ (expect no matches)`, but current repo grep returns matches in test/test_ingest_f006.py:4,41,42,237,239,536; the worker transcript/handoff shows the worker first hit those matches and then reran the grep only on test/test_deep_upsert.py.

summary: Fail: deep_upsert does not actually satisfy VAL-UPSERT-009 for values_quant/values_cat, and the accompanying tests only cover obj_desc_quant so the gap went undetected.
